blocks.content: You now have the vocabulary needed to read an Alloy configuration. You can identify attributes, expressions, and blocks; distinguish arguments from exports; trace a component reference; and recognize an invalid dependency cycle.

Continue with the type of telemetry you want to collect:

- [Use Grafana Alloy to send metrics to Prometheus](https://grafana.com/docs/alloy/latest/tutorials/send-metrics-to-prometheus/) to build a metrics pipeline.
- [Use Grafana Alloy to send logs to Loki](https://grafana.com/docs/alloy/latest/tutorials/send-logs-to-loki/) to build a logs pipeline.

These tutorials put the component concepts from this path into practice.

When you need to configure a particular component, open the [component reference](https://grafana.com/docs/alloy/latest/reference/components/). Each component page describes what the component does, the arguments it accepts, the blocks it supports, and the fields it exports.
